pr.title: nix: attempt to speed up builds
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Draft Nix tooling PR that attempted to reduce build-time closure size by switching the main derivation to stdenvNoCC and python3Minimal, installing only selected GC MWCC versions, excluding objdiff-gui from objdiff's Rust workspace build, and changing the melee install phase to emit ninja progress output. The PR was closed unmerged after the author noted fork secrets prevented cache warming and planned to recreate it from an in-repo branch.

key_files.role: Main Nix derivation changed from stdenv to stdenvNoCC, python3 to python3Minimal, and installPhase output from copying main.dol to running ninja progress into $out.
evidence_refs: pr_body:use stdenvNoCC and python3Minimal to reduce closure size

key_files.role: MWCC package install phase narrowed from copying the whole source tree to copying selected GC compiler versions under $out/GC.
Objdiff Rust build flags updated to exclude objdiff-gui in addition to objdiff-wasm.

what_changed.text: The main melee Nix derivation stopped depending on a native compiler stdenv and instead used stdenvNoCC.mkDerivation.
evidence_refs: pr_body:we don't need a native compiler

what_changed.text: The main Nix derivation replaced python3 with python3Minimal because the stated requirement was only to run configure.py.
evidence_refs: pr_body:we just need to run configure.py

what_changed.text: The MWCC derivation installPhase was narrowed to create $out/GC and copy GC versions 1.1p1, 1.2.5, 1.2.5n, and 1.3.2 only.
evidence_refs: pr_body:no Wii or ProDG compilers, or any GC tools other than selected versions

what_changed.text: The objdiff Nix build excluded objdiff-gui from the Rust workspace build, alongside objdiff-wasm.
The melee installPhase no longer copied build/GALE01/main.dol into an output directory; it ran ninja progress redirected to $out.
A bot report for GALE01 stated 'No changes' for the compared commits.
The author closed the PR after noting secrets are not visible from forks, so the cache was not being warmed up, and said it would be recreated from an in-repo branch.

smart_moves.text: The PR targeted closure-size reduction by removing classes of tools that the Nix sandbox build did not need: native compiler support, full Python, unused MWCC families, and GUI objdiff artifacts.

The author recognized that fork-origin PRs may not warm cache-backed CI paths because secrets are unavailable, and planned to retry from an in-repo branch rather than treating this slice as conclusive.

review_feedback.text: Author noted that secrets are not visible from forks, preventing cache warming, and that the change would be recreated from an in-repo branch.
Automated GALE01 report said 'No changes' for 524a9f2 to 3be74c2.

follow_up_queries: PR recreating nix build speedup from in-repo branch
fork secrets cache warming Nix CI

notes: Changed files and exact diff are well supported by loaded PR evidence.
The stated motivation and intended closure-size reductions are from the PR body; no measured build-time or download-size result is included.
There were no review comments or review approvals in the slice.
The PR was closed with merged_at null, so this should not be treated as an accepted project standard or merged source state.
The bot report supports no GALE01 output changes for the compared commits, but does not validate Nix build-speed improvement.

source_update_candidates.title: Nix CI closure-reduction attempt was not conclusive due fork cache limitations
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2715 attempted to speed up Nix builds by reducing build-time closure size: use stdenvNoCC, use python3Minimal, copy only selected GC MWCC versions, and exclude objdiff-gui. The PR was closed unmerged after the author noted fork secrets prevented cache warming and planned to recreate from an in-repo branch. Treat this as historical context for Nix tooling, not an accepted standard.
source_update_candidates.reason: May help future curators connect subsequent Nix build-speed PRs to this closed draft attempt while preserving that it was not merged or benchmark-proven.

rejection_notes: Do not promote stdenvNoCC/python3Minimal/objdiff-gui exclusion as an accepted global tooling standard from this PR alone because the PR was closed unmerged and lacks benchmark evidence.
Do not infer decompilation, naming, assembly, or matching lessons from this slice; it only changes Nix tooling files.
Do not treat the intended 'couple hundred megs' reduction as a validated result; it is a stated expectation in the PR body.
